events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario: flashing not lapped/sealed in a masonry wall, rough-complete stage. This is a defect — flashing must be lapped and sealed per TMS 402 and BIA tech notes. Decision: fail. Risk: high (water intrusion). Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant.

events.label: response

"decision": "fail",

"findings": [
"Flashing is not lapped at joints/splices as required",
"Flashing is not sealed at laps, ends, or penetrations",
"Unlapped/unsealed flashing creates a water-intrusion path behind the masonry veneer",
"Weeps may be ineffective if flashing does not direct water to the drainage cavity"
],
"actions": [
"Re-lap flashing a minimum of 4 inches at splice joints",
"Seal all laps, ends, and penetrations with compatible sealant per approved submittal",
"Verify end dams are installed at discontinuances",
"Re-inspect after correction and document with photos"
],
"rationale": "Masonry flashing must be continuously lapped and sealed to prevent water entry into the wall assembly. Unlapped/unsealed flashing at rough-complete is a non-compliant installation defect under TMS 402 and BIA technical guidance, requiring correction before close-in.",
"references": [
"TMS 402 — flashing and weep requirements for masonry veneer",
"BIA Technical Notes on flashing of brick masonry walls"
]
}
